items.title: Government Approves Additional 25 Lakh Metric Tonnes of Wheat Export
items.whyInNews: The government has approved the export of an additional 25 lakh metric tonnes of wheat to support farmers' interests and stabilize markets.
items.summary: The decision to export additional wheat is expected to benefit farmers and stabilize the market. The government has been taking measures to support farmers and ensure that they get a fair price for their produce. This move is likely to have a positive impact on the agricultural sector and the economy as a whole.
keyPoints: 25 lakh metric tonnes of wheat export approved
Decision to support farmers' interests and stabilize markets
Expected to benefit farmers and the agricultural sector
importantPoints: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is responsible for formulating policies for the agricultural sector
The Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) Act regulates the marketing of agricultural produce
The government has implemented various schemes such as the Minimum Support Price (MSP) to support farmers
India is one of the largest producers of wheat in the world
The export of wheat is expected to earn significant foreign exchange for the country
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for SSC exams as it relates to government policies and schemes for farmers and the agricultural sector.

items.title: India Emerges as a Major Hub for Jamun Development
items.whyInNews: India has emerged as a major hub for jamun development, with the country becoming a key player in the global jamun market.
items.summary: India's jamun industry has been growing rapidly in recent years, with the country becoming a major producer and exporter of jamun. The government has been providing support to the industry through various schemes and initiatives, which has helped to boost production and exports. The growth of the jamun industry is expected to have a positive impact on the economy and provide employment opportunities for farmers and other stakeholders.
keyPoints: India emerges as a major hub for jamun development
Country becomes a key player in the global jamun market
Government provides support to the industry
importantPoints: The jamun industry is a significant contributor to India's agricultural sector

The growth of the jamun industry is expected to have a positive impact on the economy
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for SSC exams as it relates to India's agricultural sector and the government's policies to support the industry.

items.title: Railways to Run Over 18,200 Summer Special Trains
items.whyInNews: The Indian Railways has announced that it will run over 18,200 summer special trains to cater to the increased demand during the summer season.
items.summary: The Indian Railways has announced that it will run over 18,200 summer special trains to cater to the increased demand during the summer season. The trains will be run on various routes across the country and will provide relief to passengers who are facing difficulties in getting tickets on regular trains. The move is expected to boost the revenue of the Indian Railways and provide employment opportunities for railway staff.
keyPoints: Over 18,200 summer special trains to be run
Trains to be run on various routes across the country
Move expected to boost revenue and provide employment opportunities
importantPoints: The Indian Railways is one of the largest railway networks in the world
The railway sector is a significant contributor to India's economy

The move is expected to have a positive impact on the economy and provide relief to passengers
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for SSC exams as it relates to the Indian Railways and its efforts to improve its services and infrastructure.

items.title: President to Visit Odisha
items.whyInNews: The President of India is scheduled to visit Odisha, where he will attend various events and meet with state dignitaries.
items.summary: The President of India is scheduled to visit Odisha, where he will attend various events and meet with state dignitaries. The visit is expected to strengthen the relationship between the Centre and the state and provide an opportunity for the President to interact with the people of Odisha. The President will also attend various cultural events and visit important landmarks in the state.

items.title: Government Reduces Jute Stock Limit to Zero
items.whyInNews: The government has reduced the jute stock limit to zero, which is expected to benefit jute traders and farmers.
items.summary: The government has reduced the jute stock limit to zero, which is expected to benefit jute traders and farmers. The move is aimed at promoting the jute industry and increasing the income of jute farmers. The reduction in stock limit will also help to stabilize the price of jute and prevent hoarding.

Move expected to benefit jute traders and farmers
Aim to promote jute industry and increase farmers' income

items.title: NITI Aayog Releases Report on Circular Economy
items.whyInNews: NITI Aayog has released a report on the circular economy, which highlights the importance of reducing waste and promoting sustainable consumption.
items.summary: NITI Aayog has released a report on the circular economy, which highlights the importance of reducing waste and promoting sustainable consumption. The report emphasizes the need for a shift in the way we produce, consume, and dispose of goods, and promotes the adoption of circular economy practices. The report also provides recommendations for policymakers and businesses to promote the circular economy.

items.title: Government Launches Scheme to Promote Divyangjan
items.whyInNews: The government has launched a scheme to promote divyangjan, which includes providing concessions and facilities to persons with disabilities.
items.summary: The government has launched a scheme to promote divyangjan, which includes providing concessions and facilities to persons with disabilities. The scheme aims to promote the inclusion and empowerment of persons with disabilities and to provide them with equal opportunities. The scheme includes various provisions, including concessions on railway tickets and reservations in government jobs.

items.title: India and South Korea to Double Trade and Launch Digital Bridge
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Lee of South Korea unveiled a vision to double trade between the two countries.
items.summary: The vision aims to strengthen economic ties between India and South Korea, with a focus on technology and innovation. The digital bridge will facilitate the exchange of ideas, talent, and investment between the two nations. This initiative is expected to boost bilateral trade and enhance cooperation in areas such as IT, electronics, and renewable energy.
keyPoints: Trade between India and South Korea to be doubled
Digital bridge to be launched
Focus on technology and innovation
importantPoints: Full form of South Korea's ruling party is Democratic Party of Korea
Related act is the India-Korea Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA)
Historical background is the establishment of diplomatic relations between India and South Korea in 1973
Key statistics show that bilateral trade between India and South Korea was $21.5 billion in 2020-21
Why it matters for India is that South Korea is a significant investor in India's infrastructure and manufacturing sectors
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC exams as it relates to international relations and economic cooperation between India and other countries

items.title: Vaishali Wins Candidates Tournament in Chess
items.whyInNews: Vaishali, an Indian chess player, won the Candidates tournament in Cyprus.
items.summary: Vaishali's victory in the Candidates tournament is a significant achievement for Indian chess, as it marks a major milestone in her career. The tournament was held in Cyprus, and Vaishali's win has earned her a spot in the World Chess Championship. Vaishali's success is a testament to her hard work and dedication to the sport, and it is expected to inspire a new generation of Indian chess players.

items.title: India to Participate in UN Global Event on Climate Change
items.whyInNews: India will participate in the upcoming UN global event on climate change.
items.summary: The event, which will be held at the United Nations Headquarters in New York, will bring together world leaders to discuss and address the pressing issue of climate change. India's participation in the event is significant, as the country is one of the largest emitters of greenhouse gases and has a critical role to play in global efforts to combat climate change. The event will provide a platform for India to showcase its efforts to reduce carbon emissions and transition to renewable energy sources.

items.title: RBI Announces New Measures to Boost Economy

items.summary: The RBI has announced a series of measures aimed at stimulating economic growth, including a reduction in interest rates and an increase in liquidity. The RBI's decision is seen as a response to the current economic slowdown and is expected to provide a much-needed boost to the economy.

items.title: India Ranks 3rd in Global Innovation Index
items.whyInNews: India has ranked 3rd in the Global Innovation Index (GII) 2026.
items.summary: The GII is a annual ranking of countries based on their innovation capabilities, and India's ranking is a significant improvement from previous years. The ranking is based on a range of factors, including research and development, entrepreneurship, and innovation output. India's strong performance in the GII is a testament to the country's growing innovation ecosystem and its potential to become a global leader in innovation.

India's ranking is a significant improvement from previous years

items.title: NITI Aayog releases 7th edition of Trade Watch Quarterly for Q3 FY26
items.whyInNews: Vice-Chairman  released the 7th edition of “Trade Watch Quarterly” on 20 April 2026 in New Delhi for Quarter 3 of FY2025-26 (Oct–Dec 2025).
items.summary: The report highlights India’s trade performance showing 1.6% growth in merchandise exports and 7.9% rise in imports, while services exports grew strongly by 7.8%. The thematic focus is on India’s gems and jewellery sector, where exports stood at $29.5 billion with a 7.8% global share in a $378 billion market. The report emphasizes structural challenges and policy priorities to enhance competitiveness and integration into global value chains.
keyPoints: 7th edition of Trade Watch Quarterly released on 20 April 2026 in New Delhi
Merchandise exports grew by 1.6% while imports increased by 7.9% in Q3 FY26
Services exports recorded strong growth of 7.8%, sustaining trade surplus
Gems and jewellery exports valued at $29.5 billion with 7.8% global share
importantPoints: NITI Aayog established in 2015, headquarters in New Delhi
No direct constitutional provision; functions as a policy think tank replacing Planning Commission
India is a major global hub for diamond cutting and polishing, especially Surat
Global gems and jewellery market size estimated at $378 billion in 2024
Sector is labour-intensive and critical for exports, employment, and foreign exchange earnings
items.examRelevance: Relevant for SSC Economics and Current Affairs — covers trade performance, export-import trends, and role of sectors like gems and jewellery in India’s economy
